p1
Build a React component that displays a full world map using Leaflet.

Requirements:

* Use react-leaflet
* Map should fill entire screen
* Enable zoom and pan
* Keep implementation minimal and functional

Return only the component code.
test

p2
Build a React component that displays a full world map using Leaflet.

Requirements:

* Use react-leaflet
* Map should fill entire screen
* Enable zoom and pan
* Keep implementation minimal and functional

Return only the component code.
test

p3
Extend the existing Leaflet map component to allow users to draw a rectangle or polygon.

Requirements:

* Use leaflet-draw or compatible approach
* Capture selected region as GeoJSON
* Log the GeoJSON to console
* Disable unnecessary tools (no circle, no marker)

Do not change existing structure unnecessarily.
test

p4
Modify the map component to store the selected GeoJSON area in React state.

Requirements:

* Save the drawn geometry in state
* Replace console.log with state update
* Ensure only one area is active at a time

Return only updated component code.
test

p5
Integrate the control panel with the map component.

Requirements:

* Store selected years in state
* Combine with selected GeoJSON
* Prepare data object for backend

Do not call backend yet.
test

p6
Integrate the control panel with the map component.

Requirements:

* Store selected years in state
* Combine with selected GeoJSON
* Prepare data object for backend

Do not call backend yet.
test

p7
Implement a function to send selected GeoJSON and year range to a backend endpoint.

Requirements:

* POST request to /analyze-area
* Send:

  * geometry
  * start_year
  * end_year
* For now, mock the response (no real backend)

Return clean API call function.
test

p8
Create a basic FastAPI backend for AUREX.

Requirements:

* Endpoint: POST /analyze-area
* Accept:

  * GeoJSON
  * start_year
  * end_year
* Return dummy structured response

Keep it minimal.
test

p9
Replace mock API call with real FastAPI backend call.

Requirements:

* Use fetch or axios
* Handle response properly
* Display returned data

Keep error handling simple.
test

p10
Implement Google Earth Engine integration in FastAPI backend.

Requirements:

* Convert GeoJSON to GEE geometry
* Fetch Land Surface Temperature for given years
* Return processed data

Keep implementation simple and efficient.
test

p11
Add logic to compute temperature difference between start and end year.

Requirements:

* Return difference map data
* Identify hotspots
* Keep calculations simple

Do not overcomplicate.
test

p12
Generate simple insights from processed data.

Include:

* Average temperature change
* Maximum change zone
* Vegetation change
* Dominant cause (based on correlation)

Keep explanations data-driven, not AI-generated guesses.
test

p13
Implement K-Means clustering for grouping zones.

Inputs:

* Temperature change
* Vegetation index

Output:

* Low, Medium, High heat zones

Keep implementation simple using scikit-learn.
test
